programme.desc: Documentary portrait of soul, jazz and R'n'B singer-songwriter Amy Winehouse, whose untimely death in July 2011 followed a well-publicised history of drugs and alcohol abuse. programme.title: Trees and Other Entanglements
programme.desc: The intertwined lives of people and the trees they love, featuring an artist refining American bonsai, a photographer observing trees and a woman fighting to protect the forests.

programme.title: Everton: Howard's Way
programme.desc: Documentary film telling the story of Everton and how they reached their their trophy-winning peak in the 1980s under the stewardship of Howard Kendall.

programme.title: The Sixties
programme.desc: A look at how American culture was changed by the Beatles and the new wave of British bands and artists that followed.

programme.title: Discovering: Vanessa Redgrave
programme.desc: A profile of the Academy Award-winning English actress, who has starred in a host of films including Julia, Mission: Impossible and Murder on the Orient Express.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and work of John Sturges, best known for directing classics The Magnificent Seven, Bad Day at Black Rock and The Great Escape.

programme.title: Urban Secrets
programme.desc: Alan Cumming explores Newcastle, discovering strange sounds by the river and finding out how traditional culture is being reinterpreted in the Byker district. He goes behind the scenes at Newcastle United with local football hero Peter Beardsley, learns about the history of adult comic Viz from former editor Simon Donald and accompanies comedienne Sarah Millican to her favourite part of the city.

programme.title: Rolling Stone: Stories from the Edge
programme.desc: Charting the history of the magazine founded in San Francisco in 1967 by Jann Wenner and Ralph J Gleason that mixed music coverage with innovative political reporting.

programme.title: Lockerbie
programme.desc: An investigation into the bombing of Pan Am Flight 103 in December 1988, examining unanswered questions and exploring the truth behind the atrocity that left 270 people dead. With access to victims' families, investigators, intelligence officers and other key figures.

programme.title: Pantani: The Accidental Death of a Cyclist
programme.desc: Documentary about the rise and fall of Marco Pantani, the maverick Italian cyclist found dead less than six years after winning the Tour de France.

programme.title: Premier League 360 - Big Sam's Bolton
programme.desc: A look at Sam Allardyce's iconic Bolton team of the early 2000s in the Premier League.

programme.title: The Directors
programme.desc: A look at the life and work of John Sturges, best known for directing classics The Magnificent Seven, Bad Day at Black Rock and The Great Escape.

programme.title: Discovering: Vanessa Redgrave
programme.desc: A profile of the Academy Award-winning English actress, who has starred in a host of films including Julia, Mission: Impossible and Murder on the Orient Express.

programme.title: The Loneliest Whale: The Search for 52
programme.desc: Documentary about the search to find a rare whale that calls out at a frequency unrecognised by other whales. This mysterious creature is believed to have lived its life in complete solitude, pointing to a greater awareness of human loneliness than ever before.

programme.title: Death on the Beach
programme.desc: In 2015 Chrissie Annesley travelled to the paradise island of Koh Tao for the trip of a lifetime. Two weeks later she was found dead in her beach hut, the latest in a tragic roll call of young travellers to have died under mysterious circumstances on this most remote of Thailand's islands. This documentary asks whether Chrissie and the others were victims of misadventure or part of a series of vio

programme.title: Death on the Beach
programme.desc: After tracking down the last man to see his daughter alive, Boyne Annesley learns that shortly before her death Chrissie was asking questions about the double murders of Hannah Witheridge and David Miller on the island's Sairee Beach in 2014.

programme.title: Death on the Beach
programme.desc: Boyne Annesley follows up leads that he hopes will prove what happened to his daughter Chrissie, and meets MP Hannah Bardell to campaign for more Foreign Office support after suspicious deaths.

programme.title: Liverpool Narcos
programme.desc: Documentary about the drugs trade in 1980s Liverpool, which turned dealing into a billion-pound business and changed Britain forever.

programme.title: Liverpool Narcos
programme.desc: When ecstasy first arrived in Liverpool, it spawned a cultural revolution - and a new breed of criminal quickly saw that millions of pounds could be made.

programme.title: Kui Buri: Thailand's Hidden Eden
programme.desc: A look at Kui Buri National Park in Thailand, a hidden eden which is home to Asian elephants and the world's largest bovine - the gaur. Once farmland, it's now a model national park.

programme.title: Wild Science
programme.desc: Tracing the brown bear's evolution from Ice Age giants to modern omnivores, and witnessing their bond with salmon and the intricate ecosystems that shape their survival.

programme.title: Loons: A Cry from the Mist
programme.desc: A year in the life of Canada's common loon, examining the perils impacting its population - from global warming and boat traffic to big wakes disturbing its habitat.

programme.title: Tusker: Brotherhood of Elephants
programme.desc: An insight into the lives of elephant bulls in Mount Kilimanjaro and Amboseli, following their drive for dominance and the subtle social bonds that form between them.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: Arizona's Paria Plateau, a true geological marvel, consisting of sweeping canyons bound by towering cliffs and fossilised dunes striped in desert red.

programme.title: Undiscovered Vistas
programme.desc: The pine forests and underwater caves of the Bahamas, housing wildlife including green turtles and rock iguanas.

programme.title: Kingdom of the Tide
programme.desc: Documentary taking a look at the intertidal zone, the point at which the ocean meets the land, and the struggle for survival facing the creatures that inhabit this realm. Their success is critical, because this is one of the most important habitats on the planet - a vital connection between worlds.

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: The deer of Hampton, New Brunswick, where sightings have reached an all-time high, leading to concerns that overpopulation may be at its tipping point.

programme.title: The Wild Wild East
programme.desc: The humpback whales and dolphins in the surrounding waters of Brier Island, Novia Scotia.

programme.title: Hope for Wildlife
programme.desc: The team must take special care when they encounter the last animal of a species. Other animals to receive Hope's help are a porcupine, a bat and a seal.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: Pregnant woolly monkey Piquita approaches her due date at Dorset's Monkey World, while capuchin Sean from Chile demonstrates his grooming skills and ruffed lemur Blue's life hangs in the balance.

programme.title: Monkey Life
programme.desc: A baby woolly monkey is tempted into the outside world by the prospect of some tasty bugs. Meanwhile, the capuchins enjoy a feast of onions, and the keepers plan a radical scheme that will allow a third meeting between young chimp Ben and alpha male Hananya.

programme.title: Orangutan Jungle School
programme.desc: The team races to a remote Indonesian village after they receive an anonymous tip off about a baby orangutan in need of rescue. Meanwhile on the pre-release island, some of the older orangutans practice independent living.

programme.title: Arctic Secrets
programme.desc: Bogs such as the Hudson Bay lowlands were once considered barren wastelands, but scientists now realise their importance in protecting the environment.

programme.title: Arabian Inferno
programme.desc: The bone dry deserts make the Arabian landscape almost inhospitable. But for three months of the year, rainfall turns a remote region into a lush paradise.

programme.title: Africa's Hunters
programme.desc: A clan of hyenas led by a powerful alpha female endures a series of challenges, while an orphaned cub ends up at the bottom of the group's pecking order.

programme.title: Wild Battlegrounds
programme.desc: How different animals cope with their surroundings, beginning with the icy heat of aggression in some of the coldest habitats on Earth, as life-threatening environmental conditions bring out predators' competitive sides.

programme.desc: A newly engaged couple loves their Florida lifestyle and wants to be on the water in St. Petersburg, which means they have to take on a renovation project.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple wants to find a property on Fripp Island, S.C., and after finding a place, demolition leads to discoveries.

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation

programme.title: Beachfront Bargain Hunt: Renovation
programme.desc: A couple looks for a forever home in Topsail, N.C., and finds a property with potential that needs major renovations; with a long to-do list and constant changes to their original design plan, the project could prove to be more than they can handle.

programme.desc: After recently renovating a rental property, a couple and their four kids are ready to get their hands dirty again on a vacation retreat.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: Mark and the guys volunteer to help a boy scout troop build a new lodge out of their old lodge while teaching them about the pioneer life.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In New England, Mark and the guys dismantle a 200-year-old barn; the barn is so big it takes extra hands to get the barn wood down and a crane to lift out the valuable beams.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The builders find creative uses for their leftovers; instead of burning the small beams, they use them to build a pavilion in which to sell their scraps as up-cycled products.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In the shadow of the New River Gorge Bridge, the builders use hand-hewn 19th-century beams to create a one-of-a-kind pavilion for the Wild Rock Community Center.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: The guys take down a massive double-pen barn in Indiana; Mark visits a lodge built from a different barn they dismantled in nearby St. Meinrad.

programme.title: Barnwood Builders
programme.desc: In Paducah, Ky., the builders try to save a little cabin in a big quarry; the cabin is in rough shape, but these guys don't give up on it; Alex makes a chair, Johnny drives a dump truck, Sherman makes a quilt, and Mark helps build a barn-wood table.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The guys tackle a cabin owned by one of their beloved subcontractors, Florida Nick; his family cabin is in need of some major upgrades; they are looking for more space on the first level and the guys build two cabin additions.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family buys a property with everything except a safe structure to call home; the team swoop in to save the day, combating a nor'easter along the way, to create a four season haven.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Ryan and Ashley buy a rundown camp on their own island; the team boats over, ready to haul materials and tackle a tricky gray water system; but after demo, island life proves tough.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: A family inherits a generational cabin they love on Sand Pond; along with the camp itself, they inherit a dark and damp building with a dated layout and lots of rot; the "Maine Cabin Masters" hammer, saw and sand their way through an overhaul.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The crew kicks off a new waterside build, but a surprise call from the homeowners could change everything; with icy delays, a custom grill pit and help from Francis, this episode is packed with challenges, charm, and unexpected turns.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The team transforms the original Monmouth fire station into a fire museum; with safety as their top priority, they deal with a messy demo, reinforce an unstable second floor with hefty steel beams and honor local heroes.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: The Bishops' waterside cottage that was built in the 1930s is overdue for an upgrade; the crew powers through demo day, upcycles some of the original lumber and expands the place upward and outward.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: While the team is in Italy, Jedi and Dixie renovate a theater for campers with special needs at Pine Tree Camp in Rome, Maine; it's a transformation from storage space to red carpet reveal as they tackle illness, weather, excavation issues and rot.

programme.title: Maine Cabin Masters
programme.desc: Ryan picks up a rundown tiny cabin for a rock-bottom price that the crew decides to transform into a customizable, one-of-a-kind retreat that they can auction off for charity.
